# UC-22.2.20 · NIS2 Management Body Accountability and Governance Evidence

## Description

Article 20 requires management bodies to approve and oversee cybersecurity risk-management measures, undergo training, and be personally accountable. This use case aggregates evidence of management engagement — board-level security report generation, policy approval timestamps, executive training completion, and risk acceptance decisions — into a single governance compliance dashboard.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIS2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes training. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Create `nis2_governance_evidence.csv` KV store with evidence types: board_security_briefing, policy_approval, executive_training, risk_acceptance, audit_review, tabletop_exercise; (2) populate manually or via ServiceNow integration; (3) alert when any evidence type is overdue by >365 days; (4) generate quarterly governance report for board; (5) include training completion certificates for management body members. Indexes required: `index=training`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: KV stores (manual governance records), `_audit` (scheduled report execution), `index=training` (executive training records).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Create `nis2_governance_evidence.csv` KV store with evidence types: board_security_briefing, policy_approval, executive_training, risk_acceptance, audit_review, tabletop_exercise; (2) populate manually or via ServiceNow integration; (3) alert when any evidence type is overdue by >365 days; (4) generate quarterly governance report for board; (5) include training completion certificates for management body members.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_governance_evidence.csv
| eval evidence_date=strptime(date, "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval days_since=round((now()-evidence_date)/86400, 0)
| eval status=case(
    days_since > 365, "OVERDUE",
    days_since > 270, "DUE_SOON",
    1=1, "CURRENT")
| sort - days_since
| table evidence_type, description, responsible_person, date, days_since, status
```

Understanding this SPL

**NIS2 Management Body Accountability and Governance Evidence (Art. 20)** — Article 20 requires management bodies to approve and oversee cybersecurity risk-management measures, undergo training, and be personally accountable. This use case aggregates evidence of management engagement — board-level security report generation, policy approval timestamps, executive training completion, and risk acceptance decisions — into a single governance compliance dashboard.

Documented **Data sources**: KV stores (manual governance records), `_audit` (scheduled report execution), `index=training` (executive training records). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **evidence_date** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **days_since** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **status** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: date, days_since, description, evidence_date, evidence_type, responsible_person, status.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (governance evidence status), Traffic light indicators (current/due/overdue), Timeline (governance activities), Single value (overdue items).

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_governance_evidence.csv
| eval evidence_date=strptime(date, "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval days_since=round((now()-evidence_date)/86400, 0)
| eval status=case(
    days_since > 365, "OVERDUE",
    days_since > 270, "DUE_SOON",
    1=1, "CURRENT")
| sort - days_since
| table evidence_type, description, responsible_person, date, days_since, status
```

## Visualization

Table (governance evidence status), Traffic light indicators (current/due/overdue), Timeline (governance activities), Single value (overdue items).
